title: × fatshedera lizei
intro: × Fatshedera lizei, also known as Elephant Ear or Fatshedera, is a rare, evergreen, flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, resulting from a cross between Fatsia japonica and Hedera colchica. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and roots have been used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, due to their perceived medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with × Fatshedera lizei include potential relief from anxiety, fever, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and cuts. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its availability and usage may be limited due to its rarity.
